name: Brightcove API
what_it_does: Comprehensive enterprise video cloud platform offering REST APIs for CMS, playback, analytics, live streaming, and media ingest, used by broadcasters and large media companies.
use_cases: Manage large video libraries with enterprise CMS API
Deliver video content at scale with adaptive bitrate streaming
Ingest and transcode media via Dynamic Ingest API
Stream live events with DVR and redundancy capabilities
Pull detailed playback analytics for business intelligence
not_for: Small teams or startups — enterprise pricing and complexity not justified
Simple video hosting needs — api.video or JW Player are simpler
Developer-first self-serve workflows — Brightcove requires sales engagement
best_when: You are a media company, broadcaster, or large enterprise with complex video workflows, high-volume content libraries, and need enterprise-grade SLAs with dedicated support.
avoid_when: You need quick self-serve onboarding, transparent pricing, or a simple embed-and-go video solution.

auth.notes: OAuth 2.0 client credentials flow; each API (CMS, Analytics, Live, etc.) requires specific OAuth scopes. Tokens expire after 5 minutes.

pricing.paid_tiers: Enterprise subscription; pricing not public, requires sales. Plans vary by bandwidth, storage, and feature set.
pricing.notes: Enterprise-only pricing. No self-serve option. Frequently cited as expensive relative to alternatives.

performance.rate_limits: API rate limits vary by endpoint; CMS API is 100 req/10s; Analytics API has separate limits

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: JSON error arrays with error codes and developer messages; multiple errors can be returned per request
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: CMS operations generally safe to retry; ingest operations can create duplicate assets if not careful
known_agent_gotchas: OAuth tokens expire in 5 minutes — agents must implement token refresh logic
Multiple separate APIs (CMS, Playback, Analytics, Live, Ingest) each with different base URLs and scopes
Rate limits differ significantly per API — agents must track limits independently
No sandbox environment; testing against production account is standard practice
Dynamic Ingest is asynchronous — status polling or webhook required to confirm completion
Error responses return arrays of error objects, not a single error

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced, OAuth 2.0 with fine-grained scopes, SOC2 Type II, GDPR compliant, DRM support
